primary_entry.label: Employees
primary_entry.purpose: Main entry point for this feature

discovery_instructions: Identify what the main item/entity is for this feature (e.g., employee record, document, invitation)
Look for list/table views - the rows represent the primary entity
Check page headers and breadcrumbs for entity naming
Note the singular and plural forms used in the UI

find_trigger: Look for buttons with: + Add, + Create, + New, plus icon
Check top-right corner of the page
Look in empty state messages for create prompts
create.validation: New item appears in list with unique identifier

find_trigger: Click on any row in a list/table
Look for 'View' or eye icon buttons
Check if rows are clickable (cursor changes)
read.validation: Detail panel, modal, or page opens showing full information

find_trigger: Look for Edit button, pencil icon
Check for inline editing (click on field to edit)
Look for status change dropdowns or buttons
Check action menus (three-dot menu) for edit option
update.validation: Changes persist after save, reflected in list view

find_trigger: Look for Delete button, trash icon
Check action menus (three-dot menu) for delete option
Look for bulk delete with checkboxes
Some features use 'Archive' or 'Cancel' instead of delete
delete.validation: Item removed from list or status changed to deleted/archived

instructions: Navigate to the feature's main section using the navigation_map
Take a full page snapshot to understand the layout
Find ALL clickable elements: buttons, links, tabs, dropdowns, icons
Map the navigation structure: tabs, sidebar items, breadcrumbs
Click each tab/sub-section and document what appears
Locate CRUD controls for the primary entity
Check for action menus (three-dot menus, right-click menus)
Document any modals, dialogs, slide-out panels
Identify form fields and input types when forms appear
Look for filters, search, sort, and export options
Check for bulk action controls (checkboxes, select all)
Note any settings or configuration options
discovery_targets: List/table of items with row actions

content_clusters.consolidation_notes: Two articles covering MFA lifecycle: login with MFA and deactivation. Both interact with Google Authenticator. Consolidated into single security management workflow.

Two articles on office location setup and assignment. First covers creation with attendance radius config; second covers assignment methods (manual and bulk). Merged into unified office location workflow.

Article on document type configuration enriched by PRD detailing expiry tracking, reminders, and multi-language support. Mobile viewing article adds manager perspective. PRD provides comprehensive configuration rules and edge cases.
Three articles covering company documentation (letters, docs, roles), asset tracking, and announcements. Consolidated as company-level record management workflows.
Article on bank account setup enriched by PRD on End of Service calculation which requires bank details for final settlement processing.

Three articles covering the complete invitation lifecycle: initial invitations, managing pending invites (resend/delete/search), and correcting wrong email addresses. All focus on HR Admin workflow for granting platform access.
Single article on creating and managing company-wide announcements with draft/publish workflow. Covers creation, editing, deletion, and role-based permissions.

Article covers viewing organization chart with hierarchical visualization. PRD enriches with employee list improvements: pagination (up to 500 employees), global search (CMD/CTRL+K), recently viewed, and role/department columns in list view.

Two articles on company customization: logo upload (visible on platform and payslips) and career page setup (brand configuration, job publishing, site builder/API options). Both are admin-level company configuration tasks.
Grouped tickets addressing gaps in bulk upload functionality and validation during data import for employee records and payroll data.
Consolidated tickets covering EOS calculation limitations, inability to reopen settlements, strict validation constraints, and gratuity field editing restrictions.
Consolidated tickets covering location constraints, job title field gaps, full name reporting issues, and custom data filtering limitations.
Single ticket addressing AI Reports inability to support employee custom fields.
Grouped tickets covering IBAN lookup gaps, legacy asset record validation errors, and incomplete employee profile recovery capabilities.
Consolidated UX issues including hire date validation timing, translation rendering, missing loading states, and document import paging problems.
Single ticket addressing inability to adjust leave balances and support negative balance scenarios.
Single ticket addressing insufficient specificity in Leave Encashments labels for end of service context.
Grouped tickets addressing data consistency issues when employee status changes, email modification restrictions, and inactive employee visibility constraints.
Grouped tickets addressing responsive design constraints and search functionality issues in employee profile views.
Grouped tickets addressing translation/localization gaps and dynamic CSM contact information insertion in communications.

product_context.title: PRD: Employee Documents
product_context.why_built: Enhance employee document management to meet compliance, business, and legal requirements. Enable creation and configuration of custom document types with expiry tracking and automated reminders. Allow HR to manage the complete document lifecycle from pre-hire through exit. Provide timely expiry notifications so managers can proactively initiate document renewals and maintain compliance.
key_design_decisions: Document type names support both English and Arabic for multi-language compliance
Expiry reminders configurable from 1-12 months before expiry (default 7 months for key documents)
'Others' document type has locked expiry toggle (TRUE) but optional expiry date entry to preserve existing behavior
Emirates ID supports both old and new formats with same field structure
Default document types cannot be deleted but can be reconfigured
Automated email reminders sent to stakeholders for proactive renewal management
Custom document types support 2-70 character names with Arabic language support
Emirates ID supports both old and new formats with quick copy action between front/back
Automated email reminders sent to stakeholders before document expiry
Document type sorting uses same logic as Company documents for consistency

product_context.title: PRD: End of Service Calculation
product_context.why_built: Enable finance managers to calculate and settle employee final settlements when leaving the company, including gratuity calculation, leave encashment, outstanding loans, and prorated salary. Automate confusing EOS calculations based on service duration, departure reason, and contract type.
key_design_decisions: Gratuity calculation uses MOL standard formula with option for custom calculation
Non-AED employees receive zero gratuity calculation
Leave encashment and unpaid leave deductions automatically calculated from time-off data
Outstanding loans and recurring deductions brought forward to final settlement
Prorated salary calculations differ based on whether departure is past, current, or future month
EOS submission locked after payment to maintain audit trail

product_context.title: PRD: Employee Records V2.1
product_context.why_built: Improve employee list management for companies with large workforces. Admins can view up to 500 employees at once (instead of 10), use global search accessible via CMD/CTRL+K from anywhere in the product, see recently viewed employees and recent searches for quick access, and view additional employee data points (role, department) directly in the list.
key_design_decisions: Pagination supports 10/25/50/100/250/500 employees per page for flexible viewing
Global search accessible via CMD/CTRL+K from anywhere in product
Recently viewed employees (up to 5) displayed before search input for quick access
Recent search terms (up to 5) shown with click-to-fill functionality
Employee list columns include name, role, and department for context
Edit icon removed from employee table rows; profile navigation required for edits
UAE-specific fields (Residence Visa Location, Trade License, MOL No.) conditionally shown based on Legal Country of Residence
DOB date picker disables future dates to prevent invalid entries

detected_integrations.description: External app integration for MFA code generation and verification during login and MFA deactivation
Employee check-in functionality depends on office location assignment; document viewing available on mobile with limited edit capabilities
Office location creation and assignment enables location-based check-in with configurable radius for attendance management
Sends invitation emails to employees, resend reminders for pending invitations, and automated expiry reminders for documents. Supports 3 automatic reminders for unaccepted invitations.
Stores employee personal data, documents, work information, and dependent details. Linked from organization chart navigation and employee directory search.
EOS calculations, leave encashments, and gratuity processing are tightly coupled with payroll period closure and validation rules. Bulk upload validation affects payroll data quality.
Provides leave balance and attendance data for employee document management and end-of-service calculations.
Outstanding loan balances integrated into end of service settlement calculations
Handles employee registration flow after invitation acceptance. Manages user account creation and login credentials.
Displays company announcements to all employees. Integrates with company menu for announcement management.
Career page displays active job openings and company information for external candidates.
Leave records created before policy unassignment are not persisted or displayed in employee profiles; document upload for leave records has file size constraints and error messaging issues.
Employee documents, company documents, and document expiry tracking are core to Records Management. Bulk operations and lifecycle management span both modules.
AI Reports, custom data filtering, and employee profile exports depend on complete and consistent employee record data. Custom fields and full name reporting are reporting-dependent features.
IBAN verification and employee bank account linkage are critical for payroll processing. Current lack of lookup tool creates manual investigation overhead.
Company assets are tracked alongside employee records. Legacy asset record handling and validation errors affect data consistency.
Performance Management module has responsive design constraints affecting table width and layout rendering in employee profile views.
Inactive employees can be mentioned in attendance comments, creating potential confusion in attendance tracking workflows.
Custom role feature supports granular access control for company assets management and other functional areas.
Welcome emails and document expiry reminders lack dynamic CSM contact information insertion and flexible notification scheduling.

check_instructions: Navigate to Automations in the left sidebar
Click + Create workflow (or similar create button)
In trigger selection, search for this feature's name
Check if this feature appears as a trigger source
Document any trigger events found (e.g., 'item created', 'status changed')
Check Actions section for actions related to this feature
Look for 'Approval' or 'Pending Approval' status in the feature
Check if items have approval workflow indicators
Look for 'Submit for Approval' buttons or actions
Find approval settings in feature configuration
Document the approval chain (who approves what)
Test approval/rejection flow if test data available
Check for approval history or audit trail

steps: Navigate to your Profile
Access Security Settings
To enable MFA: Set up Google Authenticator app and enter verification code
To disable MFA: Click 'Disable' next to Multi-factor Authentication
Enter verification code from Google Authenticator app to confirm deactivation
Receive success notification confirming MFA status change
what_to_do.expected_outcome: Employee successfully enables or disables multi-factor authentication. MFA status is updated in account security settings. If disabling, verification code from Google Authenticator confirms the action.
rules_and_constraints: Multi-factor Authentication must be pre-activated before it can be used for login
Deactivation requires access to Google Authenticator app to retrieve verification code
MFA interacts with Google Authenticator for code generation and verification
edge_cases: User loses access to Google Authenticator app - may require admin assistance to reset MFA
Verification code expires during deactivation process - user must retrieve new code

steps: Navigate to Settings > Company tab
Expand the Offices section
Click 'Add New' to create a new office location
Enter office name
Enter office location (geo-location auto-filled)
Optionally adjust attendance check-in radius (e.g., 100 meters)
Save the office location
Edit or delete existing office locations as needed
what_to_do.expected_outcome: New office location is created and available for employee assignment. Attendance check-in radius is configured to allow employees to check in within specified distance from office. Multiple office locations can be managed from centralized settings.
rules_and_constraints: Attendance check-in is limited by configurable radius from office location
Geo-location is automatically filled when creating an office
Office locations are used for employee assignment and attendance tracking
Multiple office locations can be created for a single company
edge_cases: Check-in radius adjustment affects all employees assigned to that office
Deleting an office location may impact employees currently assigned to it

source_articles.title: Creating multiple office locations for your company

steps: Choose assignment method: Manual or Bulk Upload
For Manual Assignment: Navigate to Company > All Employees
Select the employee profile
Go to Work tab
Click Edit
Select office location from dropdown
Save changes
For Bulk Upload: Use bulk upload feature to assign multiple employees at once
what_to_do.expected_outcome: Employee office location is assigned and updated in their profile. Employee can now check in from their mobile application using the assigned office location. Bulk upload enables efficient assignment of multiple employees simultaneously.
rules_and_constraints: Office location must be created before assignment
Assignment can be done individually or via bulk upload
Office location is stored in employee Work tab profile
edge_cases: Employee reassignment to different office location updates check-in location
Bulk upload may fail for specific records - manual correction required

steps: Navigate to Settings > Company
Select Employee Document Types
View all configured default and custom document types
Click 'Add New' to create custom document type
Enter document type name (2-70 characters, supports Arabic)
Set mandatory status (mandatory or non-mandatory)
Configure expiry date tracking toggle
If expiry enabled: Set email reminder frequency (1-12 months before expiry)
Save document type configuration
Edit existing custom document types as needed
Note: Default document types cannot be deleted but can be reconfigured
what_to_do.expected_outcome: Custom document types are created and configured with appropriate mandatory status and expiry tracking. Automated email reminders are set up to notify stakeholders before document expiry. HR can manage complete document lifecycle from configuration through renewal tracking.
rules_and_constraints: Document type name must be 2-70 characters
Only users with specific administrative roles can manage document types
Default document types cannot be deleted
Email reminders are configurable from 1-12 months before expiry
Default reminder is 7 months before expiry for Passport, Residency Visa, Emirates ID
"Others" document type has expiry toggle locked to TRUE but expiry date entry is not mandatory
Document names can be saved in English and Arabic
Mandatory status cannot be changed for certain default document types
edge_cases: "Others" document type: Expiry toggle is non-editable (always TRUE) but expiry date is optional
Emirates ID: Both front and back have same fields; quick action available to copy details between front/back
Reminder timing: Default 7 months may not suit all document types - customizable per type
Multi-language: Document names support Arabic in addition to English

source_articles.title: How to configure company or employee document types

what_to_do.task: View and Manage Employee Documents on Mobile
steps: Switch to manager mode in mobile application
Access Employee Documents section
View document types for your team
Check document expiration status (focuses on documents expiring within 30 days)
Select specific document to view details
Download document soft copy if needed
Note: Advanced document management actions (replace, edit, delete) require browser version
what_to_do.expected_outcome: Manager can view employee documents on mobile, prioritizing documents expiring within 30 days. Document details and soft copies are accessible. Manager is alerted to upcoming expirations for proactive renewal management.
rules_and_constraints: Only shows documents for active employees
Document counts only show expiring or expired documents within 30 days
Advanced document actions (replace, edit, delete) can only be done on browser version
Only counts primary documents of active employees
Mobile view is read-only for document management
edge_cases: Document management actions limited to browser version - manager must switch to desktop for edits
Focuses on 30-day expiration window - older documents not visible in mobile view
Only active employees shown - inactive/terminated employees excluded

what_to_do.task: Manage Company Documents and Official Letters
steps: Navigate to Letters & Company Docs section
Assign roles to team members with appropriate access levels
Upload company documents to centralized storage
Collect and verify required company documents
Generate official letters using predefined templates
Select appropriate letter template for consistency
Obtain signatory approval for official letters
Set up company bank account information (if applicable)
Track document renewals and expiration dates
Use analytical dashboards to monitor document status (if included in package)
what_to_do.expected_outcome: Company documents are centrally stored and organized. Official letters are generated from templates with proper approvals. Document renewals are tracked. Analytical dashboards provide visibility into document status and compliance.
rules_and_constraints: Analytical Dashboards are package-dependent - verify feature is enabled
Limited to selecting up to three names per dashboard
Requires accurate information input for all documents
Specific roles have defined access levels and responsibilities
Predefined templates must be used for letter consistency
Signatory approval required for official letters
edge_cases: Analytical Dashboards not included in all packages - feature must be verified
Dashboard name selection limited to three entries - prioritization required
Document upload requires verification of details before storage

steps: Navigate to Company Assets section
Select an employee for asset assignment
Input asset details: Serial number, Type, Brand, Model
Specify asset status (working, damaged, in repair)
Document purchase date
Record assignment date
Specify office location where asset is located
Save asset record
Update asset status as condition changes
what_to_do.expected_outcome: Company assets are accurately recorded and tracked against employees. Asset status is documented including condition and location. Asset accountability is maintained for operational efficiency.
rules_and_constraints: Must use dropdown menus for employee selection
Must use dropdown menus for asset status selection
Must provide unique serial number for each asset
Must provide specific asset details (type, brand, model)
Asset conditions can be marked as working, damaged, or in repair
edge_cases: Asset condition changes require status update
Employee departure may require asset reassignment or return documentation
Duplicate serial numbers must be prevented

steps: Navigate to Company Announcements section
Compose announcement message
Add images to announcement (optional)
Attach files to announcement (optional)
Choose to publish announcement immediately or save as draft
Publish announcement to reach all employees
Track announcement views to monitor engagement
Edit or delete announcements as needed
what_to_do.expected_outcome: Company announcements are created and published to all employees. Announcements can include rich media (images and attachments). HR can track view metrics to measure employee engagement.
rules_and_constraints: Announcements can be published or saved as drafts
Images and attachments are optional
View tracking is available for published announcements
edge_cases: Draft announcements not visible to employees until published
Published announcements can be edited or deleted

what_to_do.task: Configure Company Bank Details for Payroll
steps: Navigate to Settings > Payroll
Expand Bank Accounts section
Click 'Add New' to add a bank account
Fill in bank account details (account number, bank name, IBAN, etc.)
Select associated Trade License (if multiple licenses exist)
Save bank account configuration
Edit account details if needed
Add multiple bank accounts if required for different purposes
what_to_do.expected_outcome: Company bank details are configured and stored for payroll processing. Multiple bank accounts can be set up for different trade licenses or purposes. Bank information is available for salary payments and end of service settlements.
rules_and_constraints: If multiple Trade Licenses exist, users must select the appropriate license when adding a bank account
Multiple bank accounts can be added for a single company
Bank account details are required for payroll processing
Trade License selection is mandatory when multiple licenses exist
edge_cases: Multiple Trade Licenses require explicit selection during bank account setup
Bank account changes may affect pending payroll - timing coordination required
Account details must be accurate to prevent payment failures

source_articles.title: How to update your company bank details

what_to_do.task: Send employee invitations to grant platform access
steps: Navigate to Company > All Employees
Click 'Send Invite' button
Choose invitation method: individual (click invite next to employee) or bulk (select multiple via checkbox)
Confirm employee details (name, email)
Send invitation via email
Verify invitation sent successfully
what_to_do.expected_outcome: Employee receives invitation email with registration link. Invitation expires in 3 days. Employee can register and access Bayzat platform.
rules_and_constraints: Must provide valid work email (or personal email in work email field)
Invitation expires in 3 days
Email field is mandatory for invitation
Bayzat automatically sends 3 email reminders if employee hasn't accepted initial invitation
edge_cases: If employee does not register within 3 days, invite must be resent
Bulk invitations can be sent to multiple employees at once

source_articles.title: How to invite employees to Bayzat

what_to_do.task: Manage pending employee invitations (resend, delete, search)
steps: Navigate to Company > Invitations page
Search for invited employees by name or email (optional)
To resend individual invite: click purple 'Resend' button next to employee
To resend all pending invites: click 'Resend All' button
To delete an invitation: click dustbin icon next to employee
Confirm deletion or resend action
what_to_do.expected_outcome: Pending invitations are managed efficiently. Resent invitations reset the 3-day expiry timer. Deleted invitations are removed from pending list. Employees can be searched by name or email for quick access.
rules_and_constraints: Bayzat automatically sends 3 email reminders if employee hasn't accepted initial invitation
Invitations can only be deleted if employee has not yet registered
Resending resets the 3-day expiry window
edge_cases: If employee has already registered, invitation cannot be deleted
Multiple invitations to same employee should be consolidated before resending

source_articles.title: How to resend, delete and search employee invites?

what_to_do.task: Upload and manage company documents with expiry tracking
steps: Navigate to Company > Company Documents
Click 'Upload document' button
Select document file from device
Choose document type (e.g., Trade License, Insurance Certificate)
Enter document details: name, description, and expiry date
Save document
To manage: edit details, replace file, delete, or download as needed
what_to_do.expected_outcome: Company documents are centrally stored with expiry dates tracked. Admins can easily locate, update, and manage critical documents. Expired documents are flagged for renewal.
rules_and_constraints: Multiple trade license documents can be uploaded
Expired trade license documents must be updated to ensure smooth payroll processing
Expiry date is required for tracking purposes
Document type selection is mandatory
edge_cases: Trade license expiry directly impacts payroll processing—must be kept current
Multiple versions of same document type can coexist (e.g., old and new trade licenses)

source_articles.title: Storing and tracking your company documents

what_to_do.task: Configure custom employee document types with expiry reminders
steps: Navigate to Configurations > Employee Document Types
View all configured default and custom document types
To create new type: click 'Create Document Type'
Enter document name (2-70 characters, supports Arabic)
Set mandatory flag (required or optional)
Configure expiry date toggle (enabled/disabled)
If expiry enabled: set email reminder timing (1-12 months before expiry)
Save document type configuration
To edit existing types: select type and modify settings as needed
what_to_do.expected_outcome: Custom document types are configured with appropriate mandatory status and expiry reminders. HR receives automated email notifications before documents expire, enabling proactive renewal management.
rules_and_constraints: Document name must be 2-70 characters
Expiry reminders can be set 1-12 months before expiry
Default reminder is 7 months before expiry for Passport, Residency Visa, Emirates ID
'Others' document type: expiry toggle is locked to TRUE but expiry date entry is not mandatory
'Others' document type: mandatory status cannot be changed (remains non-mandatory)
Custom document types: mandatory status can be edited
Default document types: mandatory status restricted for certain types
edge_cases: Emirates ID format variations: both old and new formats supported with same field structure
Quick action to copy details between Emirates ID front/back
Multi-language support: document names can be saved in Arabic
No limit on documents per employee
Document types sorted using same logic as Company documents

steps: Navigate to Company menu > Announcements
Click 'Create Announcement' button
Compose announcement message with text
Optional: add images or attachments to announcement
Choose action: 'Publish immediately' or 'Save as draft'
If saved as draft: preview, edit, publish, or delete as needed
Published announcements visible to all employees on Bayzat app
what_to_do.expected_outcome: Company-wide announcements are created and published to all employees. Drafts allow for review before publishing. Published announcements can be deleted by authorized users.
rules_and_constraints: Only Super Admins and People Managers can create and send announcements
Announcements can only be created on web platform (not mobile app)
Announcements can be saved as drafts for later publishing
Published announcements can be deleted by Super Admins and People Managers
edge_cases: Drafts can be previewed before publishing
Drafts can be edited multiple times before publishing
Published announcements can be deleted, removing visibility from all employees

what_to_do.task: View and navigate organization structure and employee directory
what_to_do.persona: HR Admin | Super Admin | People Manager | Employee
steps: Determine user role: Super Admin accesses Company > All Employees; Employees access People tab
Click 'Organization chart view' button to switch to hierarchical view
View organization chart with departments, employees, job titles, and reporting relationships
Expand or collapse organizational nodes using directional arrows:
- Top arrow: view/hide reporting managers
- Side arrows: view/hide employees at same level
- Bottom arrow: view/hide reporting employees
Click employee names to navigate to their profile pages
Use 'How it Works' text to understand chart generation logic
what_to_do.expected_outcome: Users visualize complete organizational structure with clear reporting relationships. Navigation to individual employee profiles is seamless. Chart dynamically expands/collapses for easy navigation of large organizations.
rules_and_constraints: Organization chart only displays if at least one employee has 'Reports to' field filled out
Chart shows job titles and department assignments
Clicking employee name navigates to their profile page
edge_cases: If no employees have 'Reports to' field filled, organization chart will not be displayed
Large organizations may have deeply nested hierarchies requiring multiple expand/collapse actions
Employees without a manager assignment appear at top level

steps: Navigate to Settings menu
Scroll down menu panel
Click on 'Company settings'
Locate 'Company Logo' section
Click 'Change logo' button
Select logo file from device
Save changes
what_to_do.expected_outcome: Company logo is uploaded and displayed across Bayzat platform interface and on employee payslips. Logo customization enhances brand consistency.
rules_and_constraints: Logo must be uploaded as image file
Logo appears on platform interface and payslip documents
Logo also appears on end-of-service settlement documents
edge_cases: Logo change applies globally across all platform instances
Existing payslips retain previous logo; only new payslips use updated logo

source_articles.title: How to add your company logo to Bayzat

what_to_do.task: Set up company brand and career page
steps: Configure company brand settings (logo, colors, messaging)
Define company locations in system
Define departments in system
Set up job openings/postings
Choose career page builder method: site builder (visual interface) or APIs (programmatic)
Build career page using selected method
Publish job openings to career page
Test career page accessibility and functionality
what_to_do.expected_outcome: Company career page is live with branded appearance, company locations, departments, and active job openings. Candidates can view jobs and apply through career page.
rules_and_constraints: Company brand must be configured before publishing career page
At least one location and department must be defined
Job openings must be created before publishing to career page
Career page builder supports both visual (site builder) and programmatic (API) approaches
edge_cases: Multiple locations and departments can be configured for large organizations
Job openings can be published/unpublished independently
Career page can be customized with company-specific branding

what_to_watch_out_for.theme: Bulk Upload & Data Import Validation Gaps
what_to_watch_out_for.limitation: The bulk upload template for employee records does not support job type/employment type configuration, and the system lacks comprehensive validation checks during Excel uploads. Users can submit incomplete data (blank account numbers, MoL IDs) without proper contextual validation, creating data quality issues.

what_to_watch_out_for.theme: End of Service (EOS) Calculation & Settlement Limitations
what_to_watch_out_for.limitation: The EOS calculation framework has multiple critical gaps: users cannot self-service reopen or reverse finalized settlements (requires backend support), the system enforces strict validation preventing EOS requests for dates before the first day of the previous closed payroll period, gratuity fields cannot be edited when amounts are zero, and the overall EOS calculation logic is incomplete and requires manual intervention for complex entitlement rules.
what_to_watch_out_for.design_rationale: Payroll integrity constraints and incomplete feature implementation

what_to_watch_out_for.limitation: The platform lacks bulk document download functionality, forcing users to download employee documents individually—extremely time-consuming for large organizations. Additionally, Company Documents management capabilities are incomplete, and the system cannot automatically track or display documents based on expiration dates, preventing lifecycle-based document management.

what_to_watch_out_for.theme: Employee Profile Data Structure & Reporting Constraints
what_to_watch_out_for.limitation: The employee records system has multiple data structure limitations: work locations are constrained to officially recognized government locations (cannot accommodate project-based or emerging urban destinations), there is no dedicated job title field (forcing repurposing of generic 'Title' field), full names are always split into separate columns preventing unified name reporting, and payroll reporting lacks advanced filtering with AND logic for custom data combinations.
what_to_watch_out_for.design_rationale: Government location compliance and incomplete field architecture

what_to_watch_out_for.limitation: The AI Reports functionality does not support employee custom fields, preventing users from leveraging user-defined employee data in AI-generated reports. Engineering has confirmed this is technically challenging and not in the near-term roadmap.

The platform has multiple data consistency issues: there is no automated IBAN lookup or verification tool to identify which employee profile is linked to a specific bank account (requires manual investigation), legacy asset records with incomplete data fields (missing 'Make' field) trigger validation errors preventing modifications, and employee profile recovery is only partial—profiles can be retrieved but not all associated employee data can be restored, creating data loss risk.

Multiple UX issues affect user experience: hire date validation is enforced at the wrong interaction stage, preventing page access instead of allowing page view with deferred validation; translation rendering issues exist in the employee profile work tab (Ember frontend); the Employee Documents dashboard widget lacks consistent loading state feedback; and document import paging has inconsistent handling of page states during file deletion.

The leave management system lacks the capability to manually adjust leave balances, preventing HR from handling complex leave scenarios that require balance modifications or negative balance support. This creates inflexibility in managing edge cases and special leave situations.

The platform lacks an automated email reminder system for employees with incomplete profiles. HR administrators must manually track and follow up on profile completion, creating additional administrative overhead and reducing efficiency in the onboarding process.

The current 'Leave Encashments' labels lack sufficient specificity for end of service scenarios. The platform requires more precise terminology to distinguish between different leave encashment contexts, reducing clarity for users managing termination-related leave settlements.

The document expiry reminder system lacks flexible notification mechanisms and is constrained by its current PHP implementation. Users can only receive monthly automated emails, which may not meet all notification preferences. The system requires architectural migration to Kotlin to improve scalability and enable more granular notification controls.

The document upload interface lacks critical user-facing features including delete/remove functionality for mistaken uploads, clear error messaging for file size constraints (5MB limit), and automatic relationship maintenance when documents are replaced. Additionally, dependent document editing restrictions are inconsistently applied across different document types, creating unpredictable user experiences.

what_to_watch_out_for.theme: Employee Profile Data Entry & Bulk Management Gaps
what_to_watch_out_for.limitation: The employee profile data entry workflow has multiple constraints: avatar upload processing is slow with inadequate UI controls allowing multiple save attempts, the data conflict dialog lacks a close button restricting user control options, Emirates ID validation provides unclear error messages, and bulk role management through Excel import/export is not supported. HR administrators cannot efficiently manage complex employee role structures through bulk operations.

The platform has critical data consistency issues when employee status changes: leave records created before policy unassignment are not displayed in employee profiles, work email can be changed for registered users compromising verification integrity, and inactive/off-boarded employees can still be mentioned in comments creating confusion. These constraints represent system-level validation weaknesses in employee record management.

The platform has responsive design constraints preventing proper table width and layout rendering across different screen sizes and device types. Additionally, the 'Legal Country of Residence' searchbox in work profiles is non-functional, preventing users from effectively searching or selecting their country of residence.

what_to_watch_out_for.theme: Excel Export Failure in Team Reporting
what_to_watch_out_for.limitation: The platform currently has a technical constraint preventing successful Excel file generation and download in the View Team Page, impacting users' ability to export and analyze team data efficiently for reporting and analysis purposes.

The platform has known constraints where certain language translations may not automatically populate during company onboarding, requiring manual intervention or specific deployment configurations. Additionally, the system lacks the capability to dynamically insert CSM contact information into welcome emails, with uncertainty about the precise data retrieval method.

Employee persona has minimal reported issues (1 issue). Most limitations affect HR Admin workflows.

execution_order: 2. EXPLORATION - Navigate to feature, discover all UI elements using exploration_phase instructions
3. IDENTIFY ENTITY - Determine the primary item/entity using primary_entity discovery
4. CRUD TEST - Test create, read, update, delete for the primary entity
5. WORKFLOW CHECK - Verify if feature has workflow triggers/actions
6. WHAT_TO_DO - Execute each task from what_to_do array, validate expected outcomes
7. WHAT_TO_WATCH_OUT_FOR - Validate known limitations and edge cases
8. DOCUMENTATION - Capture screenshots of every significant state change
9. REPORT - Generate validation report with pass/fail for each task and issue
